text: The surgeon identifies the left ureter while leaving the lateral adhesions in place. Vermiculation of the bowel is visible.
The surgeon verifies the ureter's normal anatomical position before reducing the lateral attachments to mobilize the sigmoid colon. The mesenteric dissection is extended distally towards the rectum.
The surgeon identifies the estimated point of transection at the coalescence of the colonic tinea and creates a tunnel beneath the colon. The mesentery is transected prior to dividing the colon.

The robotic third arm aids in exposure as the surgeon uses a vessel sealing device to dissect the mesentery proximally, continuing until reaching disease-free colon. The vessel sealing device provides hemostasis, though a vascular stapler may be needed for high ligation or larger sigmoid artery.

The surgeon mobilizes the left colon to extend its length, allowing the proximal colon to reach into the pelvis. The intravascular injection of endocyanin green is activated, and the fluorescence lens of the da Vinci robot provides direct visualization of the colonic blood supply.

The surgeon identifies the point of transection on the colon, preparing for distal transection which may require two staple loads. If the proximal transection is incomplete with one staple load, scissors are used to cut the remaining tissue, as the staple line will be removed in the subsequent step.

The surgeon sets aside the completely freed specimen. After removing the staple line, it is discarded into the colonic lumen. The pre-placed suture is removed from the anvil, and the anvil is inserted into the proximal colon.

A purse string suture is placed at the colonic opening to prepare for anastomosis. The EEA stapler is inserted into the rectum, and its spike is deployed. The stapling device is then joined with the anvil, completing the anastomosis.

The surgeon occludes the colon proximal to the anastomosis and fills the pelvis with saline to perform a leak test. Silk interrupted sutures are used to over-sew the staple line, ensuring reinforcement regardless of the leak test results.
The surgeon removes the needles and suture material, and the specimen is extracted from the surgical field.
